title: Interbody spacer and bone plate assembly, instrumentation, and methods
abstract: Implant assemblies, systems, and methods for stabilizing a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra may include a plate member coupled to an interbody spacer with at least one fastener that extends superiorly or inferiorly from the implant assembly to anchor within a vertebral body and stabilize the joint. Inserters and methods of insertion may also be included to facilitate fixation of various implant assemblies within the intervertebral space of the joint between the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra.
claims:
Claims 1. An implant for stabilizing a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra, the implant comprising: a plate member comprising: an anterior end plate, the anterior end plate comprising an anterior surface and a posterior surface; at least one superior fastener aperture formed in the anterior end plate; and at least one inferior fastener aperture formed in the anterior end plate; an interbody spacer comprising: a posterior end; an anterior end configured to engage the posterior surface of the anterior end plate; a superior bone-facing surface extending between the anterior end and the posterior end of the interbody spacer, the superior bone-facing surface comprising at least one superior bone-engagement aperture; and an inferior bone-facing surface extending between the anterior end and the posterior end of the interbody spacer, the inferior bone-facing surface comprising at least one inferior bone-engagement aperture; at least one superior fastener sized to pass through the at least one superior fastener aperture, through a first interior space of the interbody spacer, through the at least one superior bone-engaging aperture, and into a superior vertebra to anchor within the superior vertebra; and at least one inferior fastener sized to pass through the at least one inferior fastener aperture, through a second interior space of the interbody spacer, through the at least one inferior bone-engaging aperture, and into an inferior vertebra to anchor within the inferior vertebra, wherein at least one of the at least one superior fastener and the at least one inferior fastener comprises a spike, the spike comprising: a proximal end; a distal end; and a shank extending longitudinally between the proximal end and the distal end, the shank comprising: a smooth surface extending along a proximal-distal length of the shank; and a plurality of bone-engagement fins arranged along a majority of the proximal-distal length of the shank, the plurality of bone-engagement fins extending away from a longitudinal center of the shank, wherein each of the plurality of bone-engagement fins occupies more than half, but less than all, of a perimeter of an associated cross- section of the shank taken perpendicular to the smooth surface of the shank. 2. The implant of claim 1 , wherein: the anterior end plate further comprises: at least one superior plate member ramp formed between the anterior surface of the anterior end plate and the posterior surface of the anterior end plate, the superior plate member ramp angled superiorly at a first angle; and at least one inferior plate member ramp formed between the anterior surface of the anterior end plate and the posterior surface of the anterior end plate, the inferior plate member ramp angled inferiorly at a second angle; and the interbody spacer further comprises: at least one superior interbody spacer ramp formed between the anterior end of the interbody spacer and the posterior surface of the interbody spacer, the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp angled superiorly at a third angle; and 

 at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp formed between the anterior end of the interbody spacer and the posterior surface of the interbody spacer, the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp angled inferiorly at a fourth angle, wherein the at least one superior plate member ramp abuts the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp, and the at least one inferior plate member ramp abuts the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp. 3. The implant of claim 2, wherein: the smooth surface of the shank comprises a convex curvature; the first angle of the at least one superior plate member ramp is less than the third angle of the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp such that the at least one superior plate member ramp cooperates with the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp to define a first discrete faceted curvature; and the second angle of the at least one inferior plate member ramp is less than the fourth angle of the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp such that the at least one inferior plate member ramp cooperates with the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp to define a second discrete faceted curvature, wherein the first discrete faceted curvature and the second discrete faceted curvature are configured to substantially conform to the convex curvature of the smooth surface of the shank to facilitate sliding the smooth surface of the shank along the first discrete faceted curvature and the second discrete faceted curvature. 4. The implant of claim 1 , wherein: the at least one superior fastener aperture comprises two superior fastener apertures; the at least one superior bone-engagement aperture comprises two superior bone-engagement apertures; and the at least one superior fastener comprises two superior fasteners. 5. The implant of claim 1 , wherein at least one of the at least one superior fastener and the at least one inferior fastener comprises a bone screw. 6. The implant of claim 1 , wherein the plate member further comprises: a first posterior extension plate extending posteriorly away from the anterior end plate; a second posterior extension plate extending posteriorly away from the anterior end plate, opposite the first posterior extension plate; and a plurality of anti-torsion members, each of which extends along a cephalad-caudal direction to penetrate cortical bone of at least one of the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra, wherein each of the plurality of anti-torsion members each comprises a serrated leading end that facilitates penetration of the cortical bone. 7. The implant of claim 1 , further comprising a locking plate that is securable to the anterior surface of the plate member to prevent the at least one superior fastener from backing out of the at least one superior fastener aperture and to prevent the at least one inferior fastener from backing out of the at least one inferior fastener aperture when the locking plate is coupled to the anterior surface of the plate member. 8. An apparatus for stabilizing a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra, the apparatus comprising: a plate member comprising: an anterior end plate, the anterior end plate comprising an anterior surface and a posterior surface; at least one superior fastener aperture formed in the anterior end plate; and at least one inferior fastener aperture formed in the anterior end plate; an interbody spacer comprising: 

 a posterior end; an anterior end configured to engage the posterior surface of the anterior end plate; a superior bone-facing surface extending between the anterior end and the posterior end of the interbody spacer, the superior bone-facing surface comprising at least one superior bone-engagement aperture; and an inferior bone-facing surface extending between the anterior end and the posterior end of the interbody spacer, the inferior bone-facing surface comprising at least one inferior bone-engagement aperture; at least one superior fastener sized to pass through the at least one superior fastener aperture, through a first interior space of the interbody spacer, through the at least one superior bone-engaging aperture, and into a superior vertebra to anchor within the superior vertebra; and at least one inferior fastener sized to pass through the at least one inferior fastener aperture, through a second interior space of the interbody spacer, through the at least one inferior bone-engaging aperture, and into an inferior vertebra to anchor within the inferior vertebra, wherein at least one of the at least one superior fastener and the at least one inferior fastener comprises a spike, the spike comprising: a proximal end; a distal end; and a shank extending longitudinally between the proximal end and the distal end, the shank comprising: a smooth surface extending along a proximal-distal length of the shank, the smooth surface having a convex curvature extending along the proximal-distal length of the shank; and a plurality of bone-engagement fins extending away from a longitudinal center of the shank. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein: the anterior end plate further comprises: at least one superior plate member ramp formed between the anterior surface of the anterior end plate and the posterior surface of the anterior end plate, the superior plate member ramp angled superiorly at a first angle; and at least one inferior plate member ramp formed between the anterior surface of the anterior end plate and the posterior surface of the anterior end plate, the inferior plate member ramp angled inferiorly at a second angle; and the interbody spacer further comprises: at least one superior interbody spacer ramp formed between the anterior end of the interbody spacer and the posterior surface of the interbody spacer, the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp angled superiorly at a third angle; and at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp formed between the anterior end of the interbody spacer and the posterior surface of the interbody spacer, the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp angled inferiorly at a fourth angle, wherein the at least one superior plate member ramp abuts the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp, and the at least one inferior plate member ramp abuts the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein: the first angle of the at least one superior plate member ramp is less than the third angle of the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp such that the at least one superior plate member ramp cooperates with the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp to define a first discrete faceted curvature; and 

 the second angle of the at least one inferior plate member ramp is less than the fourth angle of the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp such that the at least one inferior plate member ramp cooperates with the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp to define a second discrete faceted curvature, wherein the first discrete faceted curvature and the second discrete faceted curvature are configured to substantially conform to the convex curvature of the smooth surface of the shank to facilitate sliding the smooth surface of the shank along the first discrete faceted curvature and the second discrete faceted curvature. 11. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein: the at least one superior fastener aperture comprises two superior fastener apertures; the at least one superior bone-engagement aperture comprises two superior bone-engagement apertures; and the at least one superior fastener comprises two superior fasteners. 12. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein at least one of the at least one superior fastener and the at least one inferior fastener comprises a bone screw. 13. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the plate member further comprises: a first posterior extension plate extending posteriorly away from the anterior end plate; a second posterior extension plate extending posteriorly away from the anterior end plate, opposite the first posterior extension plate; and a plurality of anti-torsion members, each of which extends along a cephalad-caudal direction to penetrate cortical bone of at least one of the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra, wherein each of the plurality of anti-torsion members each comprises a serrated leading end that facilitates penetration of the cortical bone. 14. The apparatus of claim 8, further comprising a locking plate that is securable to the anterior surface of the plate member to prevent the at least one superior fastener from backing out of the at least one superior fastener aperture and to prevent the at least one inferior fastener from backing out of the at least one inferior fastener aperture when the locking plate is coupled to the anterior surface of the plate member. 15. An assembly for stabilizing a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra, the assembly comprising: a plate member comprising: an anterior end plate, the anterior end plate comprising an anterior surface and a posterior surface; at least one superior fastener aperture formed in the anterior end plate; and at least one inferior fastener aperture formed in the anterior end plate; an interbody spacer comprising: a posterior end; an anterior end configured to engage the posterior surface of the anterior end plate; a superior bone-facing surface extending between the anterior end and the posterior end of the interbody spacer, the superior bone-facing surface comprising at least one superior bone-engagement aperture; and an inferior bone-facing surface extending between the anterior end and the posterior end of the interbody spacer, the inferior bone-facing surface comprising at least one inferior bone-engagement aperture; at least one superior fastener sized to pass through the at least one superior fastener aperture, through a first interior space of the interbody spacer, through the at least one superior bone-engaging aperture, and into a superior vertebra to anchor within the superior vertebra; and 

 at least one inferior fastener sized to pass through the at least one inferior fastener aperture, through a second interior space of the interbody spacer, through the at least one inferior bone-engaging aperture, and into an inferior vertebra to anchor within the inferior vertebra, wherein at least one of the at least one superior fastener and the at least one inferior fastener comprises a spike, the spike comprising: a proximal end; a distal end; and a shank extending longitudinally between the proximal end and the distal end, the shank comprising: a smooth surface extending along a proximal-distal length of the shank; and a plurality of bone-engagement fins coupled to the shank and extending away from a longitudinal center of the shank, the plurality of bone-engagement fins comprising a plurality of apexes that cooperate to define a major diameter that tapers from the proximal end toward the distal end, the bone-engagement fins further defining troughs between adjacent bone-engagement fins that cooperate to define a minor diameter that tapers from the proximal end toward the distal end. 16. The assembly of claim 15, wherein: the anterior end plate further comprises: at least one superior plate member ramp formed between the anterior surface of the anterior end plate and the posterior surface of the anterior end plate, the superior plate member ramp angled superiorly at a first angle; and at least one inferior plate member ramp formed between the anterior surface of the anterior end plate and the posterior surface of the anterior end plate, the inferior plate member ramp angled inferiorly at a second angle; and the interbody spacer further comprises: at least one superior interbody spacer ramp formed between the anterior end of the interbody spacer and the posterior surface of the interbody spacer, the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp angled superiorly at a third angle; and at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp formed between the anterior end of the interbody spacer and the posterior surface of the interbody spacer, the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp angled inferiorly at a fourth angle, wherein the at least one superior plate member ramp abuts the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp, and the at least one inferior plate member ramp abuts the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp. 17. The assembly of claim 16, wherein: the smooth surface of the shank comprises a convex curvature; the first angle of the at least one superior plate member ramp is less than the third angle of the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp such that the at least one superior plate member ramp cooperates with the at least one superior interbody spacer ramp to define a first discrete faceted curvature; and the second angle of the at least one inferior plate member ramp is less than the fourth angle of the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp such that the at least one inferior plate member ramp cooperates with the at least one inferior interbody spacer ramp to define a second discrete faceted curvature, wherein the first discrete faceted curvature and the second discrete faceted curvature are configured to substantially conform to the convex curvature of the smooth surface of the shank to facilitate sliding the smooth surface of the shank along the first discrete faceted curvature and the second discrete faceted curvature. 18. The assembly of claim 15, wherein: the at least one superior fastener aperture comprises two superior fastener apertures; the at least one superior bone-engagement aperture comprises two superior bone-engagement apertures; and the at least one superior fastener comprises two superior fasteners. 19. The assembly of claim 15, wherein the plate member further comprises: a first posterior extension plate extending posteriorly away from the anterior end plate; a second posterior extension plate extending posteriorly away from the anterior end plate, opposite the first posterior extension plate; and a plurality of anti-torsion members, each of which extends along a cephalad-caudal direction to penetrate cortical bone of at least one of the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra, wherein each of the plurality of anti-torsion members each comprises a serrated leading end that facilitates penetration of the cortical bone. 20. The assembly of claim 15, further comprising a locking plate that is securable to the anterior surface of the plate member to prevent the at least one superior fastener from backing out of the at least one superior fastener aperture and to prevent the at least one inferior fastener from backing out of the at least one inferior fastener aperture when the locking plate is coupled to the anterior surface of the plate member. 21. A system for inserting an implant into a patient to stabilize a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra, the implant comprising a first superior fastener aperture in communication with a first superior bone-engagement aperture on a superior bone-facing surface of the implant, and an inferior fastener aperture in communication with an inferior bone-engagement aperture on an inferior bone-facing surface of the implant, the system comprising: a first superior fastener; an inferior fastener; an inserter comprising: a proximal end; a distal end; a shank intermediate the proximal end and the distal end; a handle at the proximal end of the inserter; and a head at the distal end of the inserter, the head comprising: a first implant retention arm; a second implant retention arm, the first and second implant retention arms configured to engage opposing sides of an implant to removably couple the implant to the head of the inserter; and a carriage configured to be positioned proximally of the first and second implant retention arms and distal the shank of the inserter, the carriage defining a first superior channel and an inferior channel that are positioned such that, with the implant removably coupled to the head, the first superior channel and the inferior channel maintain the first superior fastener in alignment with the first superior fastener aperture and the inferior fastener in alignment with the inferior fastener aperture, respectively; and a package containing at least the carriage, the first superior fastener, and the inferior fastener, with the first superior fastener located in the first superior channel and the inferior fastener located in the inferior channel. 22. The system of claim 21 , wherein the carriage is removably securable to the inserter. 23. The system of claim 22, further comprising: 

 a drill guide removably securable to the inserter in place of the carriage, the drill guide comprising: at least one drill guide aperture sized and configured to receive at least one of a drill bit, an awl, a bone screw, and a bone screw driver; and at least one drill guide angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the at least one of the drill bit, the awl, the bone screw, and the bone screw driver, as the at least one of the drill bit, the awl, the bone screw, and the bone screw driver passes through the drill guide and into the implant. 24. The system of claim 23, wherein: the first implant retention arm and the second implant retention arm further comprise inwardly extending tabs that extend into correspondingly shaped windows formed in the implant to engage opposing sides of the implant and removably couple the implant to the head of the inserter; the second implant retention arm further comprises at least one shelf configured to engage the carriage or the drill guide and removably couple the carriage or the drill guide to the head of the inserter; and the inserter further comprises a quick release lever configured to: selectively couple and decouple the implant from the first and second implant retention arms; and selectively couple and decouple either of the carriage and the drill guide from the head of the inserter. 25. The system of claim 21 , wherein the carriage comprises: a first superiorly angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the first superior fastener as the first superior fastener is urged from the carriage into the implant; and an inferiorly angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the inferior fastener as the inferior fastener is urged from the carriage into the implant. 26. The system of claim 21 , further comprising: a first driver comprising: a first driver proximal end; and a first driver distal end insertable into the first superior channel to abut a proximal end of the first superior fastener placed within the first superior channel; the inserter further comprising: a first driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the first driver retention feature is configured to engage the first driver between the first driver distal end and the first driver proximal end to guide motion of the first driver and, wherein the first driver retention feature is configured to leave the first driver proximal end exposed to permit the first driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the first driver toward the first superior channel to urge the first superior fastener toward the first superior fastener aperture. 27. The system of claim 26, wherein: the implant further comprises a second superior fastener aperture in communication with a second superior bone- engagement aperture on the superior bone-facing surface of the implant; the system further comprises a second superior fastener; the carriage further comprises a second superior channel positioned such that, with the implant removably coupled to the head, the second superior channel maintains the second superior fastener in alignment with the second superior fastener aperture; 

 the package further contains the second superior fastener, with the second superior fastener located in the second superior channel; and the system further comprises: a second driver comprising: a second driver proximal end; and a second driver distal end insertable into the inferior channel to abut a proximal end of the inferior fastener placed within the inferior channel; and the inserter further comprising: a second driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the second driver retention feature is configured to engage the second driver between the second driver distal end and the second driver proximal end to guide motion of the second driver and, wherein the second driver retention feature is configured to leave the second driver proximal end exposed to permit the second driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the second driver toward the inferior channel to urge the inferior fastener toward the inferior fastener aperture; and a third driver comprising: a third driver proximal end; a third driver distal end insertable into the second superior channel to abut a proximal end of the second superior fastener placed within the second superior channel; and the inserter further comprising: a third driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the third driver retention feature is configured to engage the third driver between the third driver distal end and the third driver proximal end to guide motion of the third driver, wherein the third driver retention feature is configured to leave the third driver proximal end exposed to permit the third driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the third driver toward the second superior channel to urge the second superior fastener toward the second superior fastener aperture and, wherein the first, second, and third driver retention features are configured to align the first, second, and third drivers in a manner that permits them to be impacted simultaneously to simultaneously urge the first superior fastener, the second superior fastener, and the inferior fastener to move distally. 28. A system for inserting an implant into a patient to stabilize a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra, the implant comprising a first superior fastener aperture in communication with a first superior bone-engagement aperture on a superior bone-facing surface of the implant, and an inferior fastener aperture in communication with an inferior bone-engagement aperture on an inferior bone-facing surface of the implant, the system comprising: a first superior fastener; an inferior fastener; an inserter comprising: a proximal end; a distal end; a shank intermediate the proximal end and the distal end; a handle at the proximal end of the inserter; and 

 a head at the distal end of the inserter, the head comprising: a first implant retention arm; and a second implant retention arm, the first and second implant retention arms configured to engage opposing sides of an implant to removably couple the implant to the head of the inserter; a carriage configured to be removably securable to the inserter, the carriage defining a first superior channel and an inferior channel that are positioned such that, with the implant removably coupled to the head, the first superior channel and the inferior channel maintain the first superior fastener in alignment with the first superior fastener aperture and the inferior fastener in alignment with the inferior fastener aperture, respectively; and a package containing at least the carriage, the first superior fastener, and the inferior fastener, with the first superior fastener located in the first superior channel and the inferior fastener located in the inferior channel. 29. The system of claim 28, wherein the carriage comprises: a first superiorly angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the first superior fastener as the first superior fastener is urged from the carriage into the implant; and an inferiorly angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the inferior fastener as the inferior fastener is urged from the carriage into the implant. 30. The system of claim 28, further comprising: a first driver comprising: a first driver proximal end; and a first driver distal end insertable into the first superior channel to abut a proximal end of the first superior fastener placed within the first superior channel; the inserter further comprising: a first driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the first driver retention feature is configured to engage the first driver between the first driver distal end and the first driver proximal end to guide motion of the first driver and, wherein the first driver retention feature is configured to leave the first driver proximal end exposed to permit the first driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the first driver toward the first superior channel to urge the first superior fastener toward the first superior fastener aperture. 31. The system of claim 30, wherein: the implant further comprises a second superior fastener aperture in communication with a second superior bone- engagement aperture on the superior bone-facing surface of the implant; the system further comprises a second superior fastener; the carriage further comprises a second superior channel positioned such that, with the implant removably coupled to the head, the second superior channel maintains the second superior fastener in alignment with the second superior fastener aperture; the package further contains the second superior fastener, with the second superior fastener located in the second superior channel; and the system further comprises: a second driver comprising: a second driver proximal end; and 

 a second driver distal end insertable into the inferior channel to abut a proximal end of the inferior fastener placed within the inferior channel; and the inserter further comprising: a second driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the second driver retention feature is configured to engage the second driver between the second driver distal end and the second driver proximal end to guide motion of the second driver and, wherein the second driver retention feature is configured to leave the second driver proximal end exposed to permit the second driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the second driver toward the inferior channel to urge the inferior fastener toward the inferior fastener aperture a third driver comprising: a third driver proximal end; and a third driver distal end insertable into the second superior channel to abut a proximal end of the second superior fastener placed within the second superior channel; and the inserter further comprises: a third driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the third driver retention feature is configured to engage the third driver between the third driver distal end and the third driver proximal end to guide motion of the third driver, wherein the third driver retention feature is configured to leave the third driver proximal end exposed to permit the third driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the third driver toward the second superior channel to urge the second superior fastener toward the second superior fastener aperture and, wherein the first, second, and third driver retention features are configured to align the first, second, and third drivers in a manner that permits them to be impacted simultaneously to simultaneously urge the first superior fastener, the second superior fastener, and the inferior fastener to move distally. 32. The system of claim 28, further comprising: a drill guide removably securable to the inserter in place of the carriage, the drill guide comprising: at least one drill guide aperture sized and configured to receive at least one of a drill bit, an awl, a bone screw, and a bone screw driver; and at least one drill guide angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the at least one of the drill bit, the awl, the bone screw, and the bone screw driver, as the at least one of the drill bit, the awl, the bone screw, and the bone screw driver passes through the drill guide and into the implant. 33. The system of claim 32, wherein: the first implant retention arm and the second implant retention arm further comprise inwardly extending tabs that extend into correspondingly shaped windows formed in the implant to engage opposing sides of the implant and removably couple the implant to the head of the inserter; the second implant retention arm further comprises a shelf configured to engage the carriage or the drill guide to removably couple the carriage or the drill guide to the head of the inserter; and the inserter further comprises a quick release lever configured to: selectively couple and decouple the implant from the first and second implant retention arms; and selectively couple and decouple either of the carriage and the drill guide from the head of the inserter. 34. A method of inserting an implant into a patient to stabilize a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra, the method comprising: providing an implant configured to stabilize a joint between a superior vertebra and an inferior vertebra, the implant comprising at least one fastener; providing a carriage; providing an inserter, the inserter comprising: a proximal end; a distal end; a shank intermediate the proximal end and the distal end; a handle at the proximal end of the inserter; and a head at the distal end of the inserter, the head comprising: a first implant retention arm; a second implant retention arm, the first and second implant retention arms configured to engage opposing sides of the implant to removably couple the implant to the head of the inserter; and a carriage housing space located intermediate the first and second implant retention arms and distal to the shank of the inserter; coupling the carriage to the inserter by placing the carriage within the carriage housing space; coupling the implant to the inserter by placing the implant between the first and second implant retention arms and engaging opposing sides of the implant with the first and second implant retention arms; and inserting the implant into the patient to stabilize the joint between the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein: the carriage is configured to receive and carry the at least one fastener, the carriage comprising at least one angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the at least one fastener as the at least one fastener is urged from the carriage into the implant, the method further comprising: pre-loading the at least one fastener into the carriage before the carriage is coupled to the head of the inserter. 36. The method of claim 35, further comprising: providing a first driver comprising: a first driver proximal end; and a first driver distal end insertable into a first channel of the carriage to abut a proximal end of the at least one fastener placed within the first channel; the inserter further comprising: a first driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the first driver retention feature is configured to engage the first driver between the first driver distal end and the first driver proximal end to guide motion of the first driver and, wherein the first driver retention feature is configured to leave the first driver proximal end exposed to permit the first driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the first driver toward the first channel to urge the at least one fastener into the implant; and applying a force to the first driver to urge the at least one fastener from the first channel of the carriage into the implant. 37. The method of claim 36, further comprising: 

 providing a second driver comprising: a second driver proximal end; and a second driver distal end insertable into a second channel of the carriage to abut a proximal end of a second fastener placed within the second channel; the inserter further comprising: a second driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the second driver retention feature is configured to engage the second driver between the second driver distal end and the second driver proximal end to guide motion of the second driver and, wherein the second driver retention feature is configured to leave the second driver proximal end exposed to permit the second driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the second driver toward the second channel to urge the second fastener into the implant; providing a third driver comprising: a third driver proximal end; and a third driver distal end insertable into a third channel of the carriage to abut a proximal end of a third fastener placed within the third channel; the inserter further comprising: a third driver retention feature positioned proximal to the carriage, wherein the third driver retention feature is configured to engage the third driver between the third driver distal end and the third driver proximal end to guide motion of the third driver, wherein the third driver retention feature is configured to leave the third driver proximal end exposed to permit the third driver proximal end to directly receive an impact urging the third driver toward the third channel to urge the third fastener into the implant, and wherein the first, second, and third driver retention features are configured to align the first, second, and third drivers in a manner that permits them to be impacted simultaneously to simultaneously urge the at least one fastener, the second fastener, and the third fastener to move distally; and applying a simultaneous force to the first, second, and third drivers to urge the at least one fastener, the second fastener, and the third fastener from the first, second, and third channels of the carriage, respectively, and into the implant. 38. The method of claim 34, further comprising: providing a drill guide removably securable to the inserter in place of the carriage, the drill guide comprising: at least one drill guide aperture sized and configured to receive at least one of a drill bit, an awl, a bone screw, and a bone screw driver; and at least one drill guide angled ramp configured to guide a trajectory of the at least one of the drill bit, the awl, the bone screw, and the bone screw driver, as the at least one of the drill bit, the awl, the bone screw, and the bone screw driver passes through the drill guide and into the implant. 39. The method of claim 38 further comprising: drilling at least one hole into at least one of the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra through the at least one drill guide aperture of the drill guide; inserting the bone screw through the at least one drill guide aperture of the drill guide; and 

 driving the bone screw into the at least one of the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra through the at least one drill guide aperture of the drill guide in order to stabilize the joint between the superior vertebra and the inferior vertebra. 40. The method of claim 34, further comprising: decoupling the inserter from the implant after the implant has been inserted into the patient; removing the inserter from the patient; and attaching a locking plate to a proximal end of the implant, the locking plate configured to prevent the at least one fastener from backing out of the implant.
